It is a binary-to-text encoding technique, commonly used in places like email systems, where binary data cannot be sent directly. The encoding process converts binary data into a format that consists of ASCII characters, making it compatible with text-based communication systems.

The Base64 encoding scheme is based on 64-character subset of ASCII characters. It includes uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and a few special characters. This subset allows for the representation of binary data in a manner that can be easily transmitted and stored as text.

The process of Base64 encoding involves grouping three bytes of binary data into four six-bit groups. Each six-bit group is then converted into its equivalent decimal value and represented by a specific Base64 character from the subset. In cases where fewer than three bytes of data are present, padding characters are added to align with the four-character pattern.

Base64 encoding ensures that binary data can be securely transmitted and stored in text-based systems, making it an indispensable tool in the realm of data communication.
